The teacher told them that one number is divisible by 6 and another by 7, and after all students said they couldn't figure out the set, they somehow could. The teacher says that in S, there's a number divisible by 6 and another divisible by 7. Then, when asked if they can figure out S, all four students say no. But after hearing that everyone said no, they all can figure out S. I need to find all such possible sets S and sum their greatest elements.

So, the key here is that initially, each student, knowing only their own number, cannot determine the entire set. But after knowing that the others also couldn't determine it, they can. This is similar to some logic puzzles where information is iteratively revealed.
